# Samuel Sindell

> Samuel Sindell is the founder of Richmond Capital Advisory, advising private investors on Dubai real estate acquisitions and cross-border tax efficiency. RERA-licensed broker. AED 300 million in transactions closed over five years. Based in Dubai, working between the UAE and the UK. Approach: evidence over enthusiasm. Every recommendation rests on data and a clear exit thesis, and he invests his own capital the same way he advises.

## Who he is

Samuel Sindell is a British real estate and cross-border wealth advisor based in Dubai. He is the founder and Managing Director of Richmond Capital Advisory. Before founding the firm he spent a decade in financial services, leading business development for a fintech and software consultancy whose clients included HSBC, BNY Mellon, Deutsche Bank and Gazprom, through the industry's shift from machine learning into artificial intelligence.

## What he does

He advises high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth investors, family offices and business principals on:
- Acquiring Dubai real estate (off-plan and completed assets)
- Capital allocation decisions based on data and growth trajectories
- Developer and infrastructure due diligence
- UAE corporate structuring as an efficient long-term holding vehicle
- Cross-border tax efficiency for clients resident in other countries
- Currency exposure management across long investment horizons

## How his approach is different

- Evidence over enthusiasm. Recommendations rest on data and a clear exit thesis, not brochures or sentiment.
- He invests his own capital the same way he advises.
- He will decline a deal he could easily close if the project does not stand up on the data.
- The precision approach: align with the city's specific growth trajectory and the intersection of location, infrastructure and developer, rather than treating Dubai as one market to simply buy into.

## What he declines

- A price per square foot too high to leave room for future growth
- A weak or unproven developer track record
- Projects that do not align with the government agendas driving the next growth phase
- Anything sold on hype rather than evidence

## His view on the market

- Dubai is not a bubble: a Tier 1 city priced at roughly a third of London, Paris or New York, with a clear growth agenda, strong tourism, stable rates and a working GDP.
- Scarcity is mispriced: under 6% of the market is waterfront and less than 1% has beach access, against more than 65% apartments. Scarce asset types in a growing market are where the right allocations sit.

## Credentials and facts

- Title: Founder & Managing Director, Richmond Capital Advisory
- Credential: RERA-licensed real estate broker (Dubai)
- Track record: AED 300 million in transactions closed
- Experience: 5 years as a Dubai portfolio manager and buyer; 10+ years in financial services
- Locations: Dubai, United Arab Emirates; London, United Kingdom
- Nationality: British

## How to work with him

- Book a private call to discuss your position
- Subscribe to his UAE real estate intelligence briefing, curated once a month for serious investors
- Email: samuel@richmondcapitaladvisory.com
